title: Procedural XML Handling
text: Procedural XML Handling Handling XML procedurally is much more flexible than Uniface's default approach. It enables you to write, read, and parse any XML data in streams and files. You can manipulate XML using Structs, or you can implement your own XML handler using the APIs provided by Uniface. For example, you can generate or read XML data required by non-Uniface sources such as web services; or perhaps you want to read or write an XML document that expresses entities as XML elements and fields as attributes; or extract and manipulate XML data that is embedded in fields. Uniface Struct functionality provides the most powerful way of transforming and manipulating XML. You can use xmlToStruct and structToXml ProcScript commands to convert and transform both XML and Uniface component structures. For more information, see Structs and Transforming Complex Data Using Structs . Alternatively, you can use Uniface component APIs to create your own SAX-like XML handler to write, read, and parse any XML data in streams and files: UXMLWRITER —generate XML streams. UXMLREADER —read XML data from a data stream or file. SAX Callback Operations —define a SAX callback handler that implements these operations to process callbacks from the XML parser executed by UXMLREADER. In addition, there is a component API for XML transformations— USYSXSLT . However, this API does not support transformations to non-XML formats. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
